mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in FEDFUNDS lowers discount rates and supports equity valuation multiples, so lower policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after FEDFUNDS declines than after flat or rising FEDFUNDS in at least two-thirds of rolling 3-year windows, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is already near zero, additional easing has limited valuation benefit and instead signals macro stress, so a low-rate regime predicts weak or negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:policy rate near the effective lower bound in 2016
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS below 1.0% produce positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if triggered hit rate exceeds 55%.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates deteriorating household income expectations and weaker demand, which compresses corporate revenue prospects and pushes S&P 500 returns lower over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-rise triggers are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of instances, or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous slowdown in RSAFS and HOUST captures weakening final demand and housing activity, and that demand contraction should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS growth turns negative and HOUST falls below its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint weak-demand condition does not outperform either proxy alone in forecasting 60-day SPX declines, or if conditional returns are not negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO indicates stronger industrial output and real activity, which improves earnings expectations and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO momentum does not correspond to higher 20-day SPX returns in rolling out-of-sample tests, or if the sign flips for more than one year.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is strong but CPIAUCSL inflation is also accelerating, the inflation component dominates and raises discount-rate pressure, so the combined regime predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 4-quarter growth is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation accelerates above its 1-year average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint growth-and-inflation regime delivers positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if its average return exceeds the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp acceleration in CPIAUCSL, especially when FEDFUNDS remains below inflation, implies negative real policy rates and rising future tightening risk; that combination should weaken S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 2.5% while FEDFUNDS < CPI inflation
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-surprise episodes do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the signal disappears after conditioning on rate level.

mechanism_narrative: In a mature expansion, a falling PAYEMS series with stable UNRATE reflects hiring stagnation before unemployment rises, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines over 2 consecutive months while UNRATE is unchanged or rises
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-stall condition fails to predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive more than 60% of the time after the trigger.
